conflicts.title: Check these rules before optimizing
conflicts.together_and_apart: {{a}} and {{b}} are set to both sit together and stay apart.
conflicts.mentor_and_apart: {{a}} mentors {{b}}, but they're also set to stay apart.
conflicts.front_and_back: {{name}} is required at both the front and the back.
conflicts.self_pair: {{name}} is paired with themselves — that rule has no effect.
conflicts.front_overflow: {{need}} students need a front-row seat but the layout only has {{have}}.
conflicts.back_overflow: {{need}} students need a back-row seat but the layout only has {{have}}.
conflicts.aisle_overflow: {{need}} students need an aisle seat but the layout only has {{have}}.
conflicts.not_enough_seats: You have {{students}} students but only {{seats}} seats.
conflicts.stale_reference: A rule still refers to a student who's no longer in the class.

app.title: SeatAI
app.subtitle: AI-Powered Classroom Seating
app.privacy_local_only: All data stays on this device — no servers, no accounts, no tracking.

guide.title: User Guide
guide.intro: Step-by-step walkthroughs for every feature in SeatAI. Click any section to expand it.
guide.section_getting_started_body:
1. Open the sidebar (menu icon at the top-left if it's closed) and pick how you want to start.
2. Easiest path: click one of the three sample classes (Small / Standard / Large) on the welcome screen — instant roster you can play with.
3. To enter your real class, use "Add student" in the sidebar to add students one at a time, OR import a CSV.
4. CSV import: open the Import section, drop your file (or click to browse). Need a template? Click "Download example CSV" — it includes every supported column with sample rows.
5. Each student needs a name. Everything else (gender, scores, language, special needs) is optional but improves the AI's placement quality.
guide.section_layouts_body:
1. Open the Layout panel in the sidebar. Five room shapes are offered: Rows (classic), Clusters (small group pods), U-shape (open in front), Circle (single ring), Custom rows (different seats per row).
2. Click any preset — the thumbnail shows what the room will look like.
3. Adjust rows and columns. The seat counter updates live; an amber "!" warns when there are fewer seats than students.
4. Clusters: pick pod size 2×2, 3×3, or 4×4.
5. Custom rows: add or remove rows individually with different seat counts (great for irregular classrooms).
6. Switching layouts clears any previous optimization — you'll need to click Optimize again to fill the new room.
guide.section_optimize_body:
1. Click the green Optimize button in the top bar (or press Ctrl/Cmd+Enter). The AI finds the best seating in under a second for typical classes.
2. The score percentage shown above the grid summarizes how well the result satisfied all your objectives.
3. Re-run as many times as you want — each run produces a fresh layout. Use the "Highlight what moved" toggle in the Results bar to see exactly who changed seats between runs.
4. Open Settings → Quality to choose Fast (1 attempt) / Balanced (3) / Best (5). Higher quality is more reliable for conflict-heavy classes.
5. Adjust objective weights (academic balance / behavioral / diversity / special needs) in Settings to bias the AI toward what matters most for your class.
guide.section_constraints_body:
1. Open the "Seating Rules" panel in the sidebar.
2. "Should sit apart": pick two students who must NOT be adjacent (rivals, distractors).
3. "Should sit near": pick two students you want close together (study buddies).
4. "Front row" / "Back row": pin specific students to the room's front or back row.
5. "On an aisle": for students who need easy egress (mobility, anxiety, frequent breaks).
6. "Near a window": for students who benefit from being on the window side.
7. "Peer mentor pairing": directional — first student mentors the second, they must sit adjacent.
8. After adding rules, click Optimize again — the AI tries to satisfy them all. Conflicts are flagged with an amber warning if a rule can't be met.
guide.section_editing_body:
1. Two interaction modes: Drag (default) and Click-to-swap. Toggle from the small icons above the grid.
2. Drag mode: grab a student and drop them onto another seat to swap. In drag mode, a single click on a student opens the AI explanation drawer (see next section).
3. Click mode: tap one seat then another to swap them.
4. Right-click any seat for a quick menu: lock the seat (locked seats survive re-optimization, useful for forced placements), unlock, view details.
5. Undo with Ctrl/Cmd+Z, redo with Ctrl+Y or Ctrl+Shift+Z. Every manual change is reversible.
6. Keyboard navigation: Arrow keys move the selection between seats, Enter opens the detail drawer for the selected student, Escape clears the selection.
guide.section_understanding_title: Understanding why a student sits there
guide.section_understanding_body:
1. Click any student — in the grid or the sidebar list — to open the AI Reasoning drawer on the right (a bottom sheet on mobile).
2. The drawer shows the student's profile, special needs, and teacher notes.
3. "Why this seat" lists the rules the AI satisfied: front row for vision, near window per your rule, aisle for mobility, etc.
4. "Strengths" highlights qualitative wins: peer academic balance with neighbors averaging X, friend adjacency, gender diversity, mentor pairing.
5. "Tradeoffs to watch" surfaces compromises: incompatible peer adjacent, all-same-gender pod, mentor pair separated.
6. "Adjacent peers" shows each neighbor with their relation (Friend / Conflict / Mentor / Mentee / Neutral) and academic/behavior gaps.
7. The Confidence chip (Strong fit / Acceptable / Compromise) summarizes how well the placement satisfied hard rules.
8. "🔄 Last sat together" badges show how recently this pair was adjacent in past runs — useful for rotation planning.
guide.section_export_body:
1. After running an optimization, the Export menu (top-right) is enabled.
2. PNG: image of the seating chart, useful for slides or messaging.
3. PDF: A4 landscape with title, score, and the chart fitted to one page; warnings (if any) appear on a second page.
4. CSV: one row per seat with the student's full profile — open in Excel/Sheets, share with admin staff.
5. JSON: full structured snapshot (layout + students + result + warnings) for power users.
6. Print button (next to the score chip): opens a print-optimized view. The browser's native print dialog handles the rest.
7. All exports happen entirely in the browser — nothing is uploaded.
guide.section_personalize_body:
1. Open the Add/Edit Student form. The first field is a photo placeholder — click it to upload an image from your device.
2. The photo is automatically resized to ~30 KB so storage stays small even with 40 students.
3. The photo replaces the colored initial circle in every place a student appears (grid, sidebar, detail drawer, neighbor cards).
4. Below the form there's a "Teacher notes" textarea — write private observations like "struggles after lunch", "helps neighbors with reading", etc.
5. Notes appear at the top of the AI reasoning drawer so they're visible whenever you tap that student.
6. Photos and notes are stored only on your device — never uploaded.
guide.section_ai_body:
1. SeatAI's built-in reasoning is rule-based and free. If you want fully natural-language paragraphs explaining each placement, you can plug in a language model.
2. Open Settings → AI explanations and check "Enable AI explanations".
3. Get an API key from console.anthropic.com (you'll need an Anthropic account).
4. Paste the key into the field. The eye icon toggles visibility for verification; the trash icon next to it forgets the key from this browser.
5. Pick a model: Haiku 4.5 (fast and cheap, ~$0.001 per generation) or Sonnet 4.6 (slower, more nuanced).
6. Open any student's reasoning drawer and click "Generate AI explanation" — a paragraph appears at the top within a few seconds.
7. Important: the API key stays only in your browser; calls go directly from your browser to Anthropic. Don't enable on a shared computer.
8. Privacy note: when you generate an AI explanation, the student's name and your teacher notes for that student are sent to Anthropic as part of the prompt. If a student's notes contain sensitive medical, legal, or family details, either keep AI off for that student or trim the notes first.
guide.section_projects_body:
1. Open the Projects panel in the sidebar.
2. "Save current class" creates a named snapshot of your roster, layout, rules, weights, and last optimization result.
3. Switch between projects with one click — everything restores including the last optimization.
4. Delete projects you no longer need.
5. Projects live in your browser's storage — clearing browser data deletes them. Use the JSON export as a backup.
guide.section_accessibility_body:
1. Theme picker (sun/monitor/moon icons in the header): choose Light, Dark, or Follow System (auto-switches with your OS).
2. Text size picker (A− / A / A+) bumps every text in the app — useful when projecting to a class or for low-vision teachers.
3. Language: switches the entire UI immediately. Hebrew, Arabic, English, Russian. RTL layout activates automatically for Hebrew and Arabic.
4. Keyboard navigation: Arrow keys to move between seats, Enter to open student details, Escape to clear selection, Ctrl+Z/Y for undo/redo, Ctrl+Enter to optimize.
5. Reduced motion is respected if your OS has it enabled — no spinning animations, instant transitions.
6. The app installs as a Progressive Web App: in Chrome/Edge, click the install icon in the address bar; on iPad, use Share → "Add to Home Screen". Once installed it works fully offline.

tips.title: Quick tips
tips.optimize: Click Optimize to let the AI rearrange your class. Hit it as many times as you want — every run finds a fresh layout.
tips.click_student: Click any student — in the grid or the sidebar list — to see why the AI placed them there.
tips.layouts: Try other room shapes from the Layout panel: clusters, U-shape, circle, or your own custom rows.
tips.lock_seat: Right-click a seat to lock it. Locked seats stay put when you re-optimize — useful for forced placements.
tips.keyboard: Use Arrow keys to move the selection between seats, Enter to inspect a student, Escape to clear.
tips.constraints: Open the Seating Rules panel to mark friends, conflicts, front-row needs, aisle / window preferences, and mentor pairs.

detail.no_result_yet: Run an optimization to see this student's placement analysis.
detail.rotation_tooltip: Sat adjacent {{count}} times in your recent runs

explain.not_placed: This student wasn't placed — your class has more students than seats.
explain.slot_not_found: This student's seat couldn't be matched to the current layout.
explain.front_for_mobility: Placed in the front row for mobility access.
explain.front_for_need: Placed in the front row because front-row seating is required.
explain.front_for_sensory: Placed in the front row to support a vision or hearing need.
explain.front_by_rule: Pinned to the front row by your teacher rule.
explain.front_by_rule_unmet: Your front-row rule couldn't be satisfied here — consider locking a front seat.
explain.back_by_rule: Placed in the back row by your teacher rule.
explain.aisle_by_rule: On an aisle column as you requested.
explain.window_by_rule: Seated near the window per your rule.
explain.quiet_edge: Seated on an edge to give a quieter zone.
explain.peer_academic_balance: Academic balance — neighbors average ~{{avg}}, close to this student's level (good for peer tutoring).
explain.peer_behavior_balance: Behavior is stable here — neighbors average ~{{avg}}, similar profile.
explain.mentor_paired: Paired with mentor/mentee {{name}} as you requested.
explain.bilingual_support: Bilingual peers nearby — supports language access.
explain.incompatible_adjacent: An incompatible peer ({{name}}) is adjacent — monitor closely or split them.
explain.front_need_unmet: This student needs the front row but didn't get there — consider a manual lock.
explain.aisle_unmet: Your aisle rule for this student wasn't satisfied.
explain.window_unmet: Your near-window rule for this student wasn't satisfied.
explain.mentor_pair_unmet: Mentor pairing with {{name}} couldn't be placed adjacent.
explain.gender_homogeneous: All neighbors share this student's gender — diversity is weaker here.

layout.features_hint: Tap a cell to cycle it: seat → obstacle → teacher's desk. Blocked cells are left empty when seating students.
layout.title: Classroom Layout
layout.u_shape_desc: Open in front, walls on three sides.
layout.not_enough_seats: Not enough seats for the current class.
layout.not_enough_seats_detail: This layout has {{seats}} seats but you have {{students}} students. Add more rows or change layout.

constraints.title: Seating Rules
constraints.need_more_students: Add at least 2 students to define seating rules.
constraints.must_separate_desc: These students will be placed far apart from each other.
constraints.should_sit_near_desc: These students will be placed in adjacent seats when possible.
constraints.force_front_row_desc: These students will be placed in the front row.
constraints.prefer_back_row_desc: These students will be placed in the back row.
constraints.on_aisle_desc: Place these students on an edge column (easy egress).
constraints.near_window_desc: Place these students on the window side of the room.
constraints.peer_mentor_desc: First student mentors the second — they must sit adjacent.
constraints.conflicts_heading: Some rules conflict with each other
constraints.has_conflicts: Some rules conflict — review them before optimizing.
constraints.conflict_pair_both: {{a}} and {{b}} are in both 'separate' and 'sit near' lists.
constraints.conflict_front_and_back: {{name}} is assigned to both the front and back row.
constraints.conflict_back_vs_accessibility: {{name}} requires the front row but is assigned to the back.

students.title: Students
students.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this student?
students.empty: No students yet. Add your first student to get started.
students.name: Name
students.photo_error_type: Use JPEG, PNG, WebP, or GIF.
students.photo_error_decode: Couldn't read that image — try a different file.
students.notes_placeholder: Private observations, e.g. "needs help focusing after lunch". Stays on this device only.
students.need_more_students: Add at least 2 students to define seating rules.
students.add_students_or_import: Add students or import a CSV below

classroom.drag_hint: 🖱️ Drag students to swap seats — right-click a seat for options
classroom.click_hint: 👆 Click a student to select, then click a destination to swap

optimization.title: Optimization
optimization.show_movement_diff: Highlight students who moved from the previous run

explanation.title: Why This Arrangement?
explanation.overview: This arrangement was optimized based on the following factors:
explanation.academicGood: Good academic balance - students with similar levels are grouped for peer support
explanation.behavioralGood: Behavioral compatibility - compatible students are seated together
explanation.diversityGood: Good diversity - mixed genders promote inclusive environment
explanation.specialNeedsGood: All special needs accommodations are met

settings.title: Settings
settings.weights_hint: Higher means the optimizer cares more about it. These are independent priorities, not a split of 100%.
settings.preset_balanced_desc: Weigh academics, behaviour, diversity and special needs evenly.
settings.preset_behavior_desc: Prioritise spreading out behaviour for a calmer room.
settings.preset_academic_desc: Prioritise mixing ability levels so students support each other.
settings.preset_inclusion_desc: Prioritise special-needs placement (front row, quiet areas).
settings.quality_hint: Higher quality runs the optimizer several times and keeps the best result. Recommended for conflict-heavy classes.
settings.rotation_hint: When on, re-running the optimizer avoids seating students next to neighbors they recently sat with, so the room rotates over time.
settings.ai_description: Use a language model to write a teacher-friendly paragraph for each student. Your API key stays in this browser; the AI call goes directly to Anthropic.
settings.ai_security_note: Your key is stored only in this browser. Don't enable on a shared computer. Each generation costs roughly $0.001.
settings.need_more_students: Add at least 2 students to define seating rules.
settings.weightsDesc: Adjust the importance of each optimization factor

compare.title: Compare arrangements
compare.hint: Generate an alternative arrangement with the same settings and compare it side by side. Nothing changes until you accept it.

projects.backup_hint: Saves everything (students, projects, settings) to a single .json file so you can keep a copy off-browser.
projects.restore_confirm_body: This will replace your current students and projects with the backup from {{date}} ({{students}} students, {{projects}} projects).
projects.restore_error_invalid_json: Couldn't read the file (not valid JSON).
projects.restore_error_wrong_schema: This file isn't a SeatAI backup.
projects.restore_error_unsupported_version: This backup was made by a newer version of SeatAI.
projects.restore_error_missing_fields: The backup file is missing required data.
projects.title: Projects
projects.no_projects: No saved projects yet. Add students and click Save.
projects.deleteConfirm: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.

onboarding.title: Welcome to SeatAI!
onboarding.subtitle: Let's get your classroom set up
onboarding.welcome_desc: Intelligent classroom seating — balancing academic levels, behavior, social dynamics, and accessibility requirements automatically.
onboarding.import_desc: Upload a CSV file with student names and attributes — done in seconds.
onboarding.manual_desc: Fill in names, scores, relationships, and accessibility needs one by one.
onboarding.optimize_desc: The AI engine generates an optimal seating chart in under a second.
onboarding.export_desc: Get a ready-to-hang seating chart as a PDF or PNG.
onboarding.privacy: All data stays in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.
onboarding.step2Desc: Add your students manually or import from CSV
onboarding.step3Desc: Adjust optimization weights to match your priorities

csvImport.title: Import CSV
csvImport.drop_here: Drop a CSV file here or browse
csvImport.error_no_header: CSV must have a header row and at least one data row.
csvImport.warn_invalid_value: Row {{row}}: "{{value}}" isn't a valid {{column}} — used "{{fallback}}" instead
csvImport.warn_invalid_score: Row {{row}}: {{column}} "{{value}}" isn't a number — used the default
csvImport.warn_duplicate_name: Duplicate name "{{name}}" on rows {{first}} and {{second}} — both imported
csvImport.warn_too_many_rows: More than {{max}} rows — extra rows were ignored

explanation.title: Placement Explanations
explanation.subtitle: Why each student was placed where they are
explanation.reason_front_row_teacher: Front row assigned by teacher rule
explanation.reason_back_row_teacher: Back row assigned by teacher rule
explanation.reason_quiet_area_back: Quiet area: placed in back rows
explanation.reason_quiet_area_not_met: Quiet area preference not fully met

gridControls.show_relations: Show friend & conflict lines on hover

googleImport.title: Google Classroom
googleImport.not_configured: Add a Google OAuth client ID (VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID) to enable one-click roster import from Google Classroom.
googleImport.no_courses: No active classes found for this account.
googleImport.privacy_note: Runs in your browser — student names go from Google straight to this device, never to a SeatAI server.
